rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BERTA CONSUELO LOARCA-HERRERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/27/23 to be heard with the related trust accounting. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (2) The amount of transfer from this estate to the trust appears to be incorrect on Schedule C-1: there are two different amounts shown for the transfer on 12/19/22, resulting in different totals. (3) Note 1 on Schedule 1 refers to a RMD of $10,318.61 on 12/31/19 shown in the trust accounting (and also third party and plan administration fees of $143.54), but it is not clear where in the trustee's second accounting those figures were shown. Similarly, Schedule E shows a $10,186.86 distribution on 12/31/19 (or 12/31/21 as shown on Schedule J?) that will need to be confirmed with the trust accounting (plus $13.98 third party and plan administration fees) but there is no Note 2 on either Schedule E or F. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDMUND SHEFFIELD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/26/24. (No earlier date available.) (1) Will the interest overpaid to Marilyn Rockcastle be made up to the residuary beneficiaries since it apparently cannot be reclaimed from her (or her estate?). (2) The full explanation from Voya about the difference between the annuity payments received after death and the amount reimbursed on 12/17/15 has not yet been filed. (3) Was Kim Schwarcz or her attorney unable to generate another printout of the invoice for $1,098.92? (4) There were 280.383 shares of Allstate Corporation inventoried and appraised at $20,006.73, but 286 shares were reportedly sold on 1/31/17. Likewise, there were 176.924 shares of First Energy inventoried, but 180 shares sold on 1/31/17. See also Sempra Energy and Devon Energy. (5) The accounting does not have an informational schedule showing changes in the form of assets, such as purchases or reinvested dividends. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] (6) Where did the 65 shares of Alcatel-Lucent and 36 shares of Eastman Chemical come from? (7) What happened to all of the savings bonds? (8) For what services was Boxer Group paid $3,000 on 8/28/15? The detailed schedules in support of an accounting must show the date, the source or payee, the description, and the amount. This also includes âfiling feesâ not sufficiently described as to what was filed. (9) Reimbursements to the executor for "expenses paid" must be fully itemized. (10) The total amount of receipts was misstated in paragraph 3 of the petition. (11) Assets no longer on hand should not be included on the schedule of property on hand at the end of an accounting. (12) No statement has been filed to verify the securities on hand or the $196.26 still in the Wells Fargo account 6143 as of 5/19/17. (13) Why was there an uncashed check from Strategic Global Fund on hand as of 5/19/17? (14) Costs of sale are not usually deducted in the gains or losses schedule. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/14/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ERMA L. ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Reducing Bond
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Bond will be reduced to $986,000 based on the market value of liquid assets on hand at the end of the last accounting ($928,686.92), the annual receipts shown in the last accounting ($24,271.16), and reduced by the fees and costs allowed for the last accounting ($63,864.00), or a bondable estate of $889,094.08. (Bank statements showing the current balances are meaningless without a supporting, verified accounting showing how estate assets were managed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALIREZA TABESH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/05/2024. The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Waiver of accounting to be signed by Leona on or after her 18th birthday unless Petitioner presents that Kiyoka has the legal authority (i..e. court appointed guardian of estate of Loena) to waive accounting on behalf of Leona. Prob. Code 10954(b)(2). (2) Clarification of the total Inventory and Appraisal need of the "supplemental" Final Inventory and Appraisal filed on 7/19/2022. There was previously filed "corrected" final Inventory and Appraisal. If the assets belonging to the estate are listed on the later filed Inventory and Appraisal, not the previously filed one, the 7/19/2022 I&A should have been filed as corrected I&A. (3) Allocation of personal representatives' statutory compensation. Petition seeks 100% of the statutory compensation to be paid to the successor PR only with no written consent from the former personal rep. Prob. Code 10805. (4) Proposed distribution needs to specify the dollar amount to be distributed to each heir. Exhibit D merely shows the balance of the account. CRC 7.651(b). (5) Proposed distribution needs to specify to whom the distribution that the minor heir. Petition is silent. Outright distribution to a minor heir will not be approved. Petitioner may consider filing an amendment to petition or an amended petition after Leona turns 18. (6) Allowance or Rejection of Creditor's Claim (for the claim filed State Agency) [Form DE-174] or a written satisfaction of the claim by the creditor to be filed. (7) Proposed order to be revised accordingly. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For The Court To Limit The Executors Authority, And Executor To Obtain The Additional $11,000,000.00 Bond To Cover The Estate'S Interest In Decedent'S Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. a) A Proof of Service by First-Class Mail - Civil form was filed on 10/24/23, not the proof of service of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing (DE-120) required for a decedent's estate proceeding. b) That proof of service shows that notice of the motion was mailed on 10/12/23. The motion was not filed until 10/24/23. c) That proof of service does not have a copy of the proper notice or the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it. d) Although the executor filed an ex parte petition on 2/14/23 to limit his authority pending additional bond, a copy of it with a proposed order was apparently never dropped off, as required by LRSF 14.11 B., as it was never logged in by the clerk on the spreadsheet that tracks drop-off ex parte petitions for it to then be assigned to an examiner. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY NGAI YING CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) A Supplemental I&A shows an additional $10,724.53 in cash. This executor's receipt filed on 10/16/23 in case PES-19-302573 shows fiduciary income tax reportedly paid after the administrator of Frank Chew's estate (this executor) was authorized to distribute that estate. (No support for the taxes have been filed.) 2) The verification of the petition is incomplete (incomplete date). 3) The proof of service filed on 12/7/22 shows that notice was mailed to Ming Yu Wu as the trustee of the Nancy Ngai Ying Chew Trust and to James Chew as "Beneficiary," but a review of earlier papers shows that the executor, when petitioning for appointment, claimed that James Chew (son), Yan Miao Wu (niece), and Ming Yu Wu (nephew/Executor) were all successor trustees of the Nancy Ngai Ying Chew Trust. The executor must clarify who the trustee of the devisee trust is and why James Chew is no longer a trustee. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESUSITA A. DOMATOL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the full disclosure required by Probate Code Section 9202(b) was not made. An heir is not necessarily a beneficiary and a beneficiary is not necessarily an heir. [Probate Code Sections 24 and 44.] Decedent's only heir is her surviving sister. Her niece is a BENEFICIARY under the will, not an heir. A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify her OWN pleadings, including and supplements or amendments; attorney statements are NOT necessary or correct. The executor maintained cash well in excess of the FDIC insured amount at one bank, which action will not be approved. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] The footer must contain the title of the paper or some clear and concise abbreviation. [CRC 2.110(b).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PINKEY BROUSSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Leslie M. Byrne's Petition Continued to 2/14/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) A proposed personal representative is required to sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. An attorney "declaration" is not sufficient. [CRC 7.103(c).] (2) Petitioner is the administrator of Decedent's post-deceased granddaughter Pinkey Gibson's estate. Pinkey Gibson's heirs and their relationship to this decedent, ages, and addresses must be set forth in a supplement, signed and verified by the proposed personal representative (not in another "declaration" by the attorney). Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PINKEY BROUSSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Cynthia's Corrected Petition Continued to 2/14/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) The proof of service filed on 12/7/23 does not show on the face of the notice that a CORRECTED petition has been filed. (2) It also shows that notice was mailed on 12/4/23, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (3) No proof of publication of the CORRECTED PETITION has been filed yet. (4) Notice mailed to more than one person in the same household must be sent separately to each person. [CRC 7.51(a)(3).] (5) No supplement has been filed yet to clarify the relationship to the decedent of Robert Gibson and Tanya Gibson. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSE BREWSTER LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Validity Of 2013 Trust Instrument As Restatement Of 2000 Trust; For Appointment Of Successor Trustee; And To Determine Entitlement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) This is essentially a Prob. C. Sect. 850 petition. Prob. C. Sect. 850 requires a beneficiary of the trust to receive mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] (3) The only correct form of notice to claim property under Prob. C. Sect. 850 is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. (4) Notice must also include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (5) Has Sharon Jones consented to be named as an alternate successor trustee? (6) The copy of the petition received by the examiner does not appear to be a true and complete copy: there is no Exhibit B attached to it. (7) Why would the Court need to confirm authority to sell the real property? If the petitioner is appointed as the successor trustee, would she not be empowered by the trust to do so? (8) Part 10 of the 2013 trust gave "the trust property" (not identified) outright to David T. Brewster, who survived. Please provide a proposed order and true and complete courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/14/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDO J FRUCTUOSO JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/22/2024. The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Need Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E. (2) Waivers of Bond signed by Ed and by Isabel need to be dated. (3) Nominations signed by Ed and by Isabel need to be dated. (4) Petition caption does not set forth IAEA authority being sought. CRC 7.102. (5) Petition #8 to identify petitioner's parent who is related to the decedent. (6) Notice of directions for remote appearance to be given to all persons entitled to notice. LRSF 14.1.C. 6). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
